steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize a similarity between the two books. The decisive clue in the notes is that both books entered the public domain — The Prophet in 2019 and The Professor’s House in 2021. A correct answer must frame both books as sharing this status, not contrast them or focus on just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘…entered the public domain in 2019, unlike Willa Cather’s novel …’: the word “unlike” signals a contrast, not a similarity — this directly contradicts the student’s goal. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘…recently entered the public domain’: both books are named as joint subjects of “entered the public domain,” directly expressing the shared status and satisfying the similarity goal. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘…a collection of prose poems, whereas … is a novel’: “whereas” signals a contrast in genre, not a similarity in public domain status. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The year was 2021, and the copyright to Willa Cather’s … had finally expired’: this mentions only one book’s public domain entry; it ignores The Prophet entirely and cannot express a similarity between the two. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
